title: Goldman, BofA See 2024 Flashback in China Before Politburo Meets
summary: As the calendar turned to July, Chinese retail sales were decelerating fast, the stock market was in the doldrums and investment unexpectedly turned for the worse. Public spending shrank as local governments struggled with financing.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 24
summary: Brent crude retreated after hitting $100 a barrel in the previous session on concern over widening risks to Middle East supplies, as US President Donald Trump’s revived trade tariffs posed economic headwinds.

title: Tanker Exits Red Sea Dark as China Ship Heads Toward Strait
summary: A Greek tanker carrying Saudi crude exited the Red Sea with its transponder off, while another Chinese vessel and Russian cargoes headed toward the Bab el-Mandeb strait, after Houthi attacks heightened risks.

title: Trump to Take Up Manila’s Concerns With Xi, Philippines Says
summary: US President Donald Trump assured Philippine leader Ferdinand Marcos Jr. that he would take up Manila’s concerns when he meets with Chinese President Xi Jinping, the Southeast Asian nation said.

title: Stocks Get Some Relief as Brent Slips Below $100: Markets Wrap
summary: US stocks are finding some respite after posting their biggest drop this month as Brent crude eased below $100 a barrel and bond yields halted their ascent.

title: Japan Is Whipsawing Wall Street’s CLO Machine, Study Shows
summary: The $1.3 trillion US CLO market is taking its cues from Tokyo as much as Wall Street.

title: Mauritius Tax Wave Threatens to Erode Its Banks’ Profitability
summary: A raft of new corporate taxes that could erode banks’ profitability is testing Mauritius’s long-standing status as a low-tax gateway for cross-border capital, according to Moody’s Ratings.

title: PIC: Why South Africa’s Biggest Fund Manager Is in Turmoil
summary: The South African firm that oversees state workers’ pensions has been plunged into a fresh crisis triggered by the suspension of its chief executive and the resignation of its chairman.

title: Victoria Plc Slumps to £326 Million Loss as Debt Deal Bites
summary: Victoria Plc reported a wider annual loss as the troubled British carpetmaker counted the cost of its recent debt refinancing.

title: Zara’s Ortega Buys Paris Office Complex for About €800 Million
summary: Zara billionaire Amancio Ortega bought a business center in Paris from Invesco Ltd., in what is his biggest transaction in Europe.

title: Dangote Prefers London Over Dubai for Listing of Cement Unit
summary: Dangote Cement Plc prefers to list its cement business in London because it will be quicker to sell shares on the exchange in the UK than in Dubai, according to Mariya Dangote, a board member in the cement business.

title: How Franchise Restaurants Opened the Door to the Gig Economy
summary: The ruthless labor history of your favorite fast food joint.

title: Odd Lots: How Franchising Led to the Gig Economy (Podcast)
summary: When the fast food industry began booming in the 1950s, it did so via a new business model known as the franchise. This model allowed independent operators to license trademarks from a business like McDonald’s or Dunkin Donuts, and it soon spread across the country, with huge consequences for how Americans are employed. Legal battles fought by franchises eventually opened the door to what’s now known as the gig economy, allowing Uber drivers to be treated in much the same way as the operator of

title: Khazanah Is Said to Sell $340 Million Tenaga Stake in Placement
summary: Malaysian sovereign wealth fund Khazanah Nasional Bhd. has sold 1.39 billion ringgit ($340 million) worth of shares in state-owned power firm Tenaga Nasional Bhd., according to people with knowledge of the matter.

title: Chinese Banks Break With Lending Benchmark in Pricing New Credit
summary: Three of the biggest Chinese banks experimented with anchoring new loans to interbank rates, a step toward a new pricing mechanism that could better reflect supply and demand for money in the market.

title: Malaysia May Again Host Formula One Racing as Iran War Puts Bahrain in Doubt
summary: Malaysia could host a Formula One race for the first time in nine years, potentially replacing the Bahrain Grand Prix as the Middle East conflict drags on, according to the country’s Youth and Sports Minister Mohammed Taufiq Johari.

title: Singapore Pushes Back as US Levies Hit on Forced-Labor Claim
summary: Singapore’s top diplomat said there was no economic justification for the US to impose tariffs on the city-state, adding that it seeks to avoid becoming “collateral damage” in Washington’s broader trade policy.

title: South Africa Seeks Private Capital to Tackle Water Crisis
summary: South Africa plans to tap private investors to tackle the country’s water crisis by developing blended-finance projects and accelerating a pipeline of bankable infrastructure investments.

title: Japan 40-Year Yield Surges as Traders Question BOJ on Inflation
summary: Japan’s 40-year government bond yield led gains across maturities, underscoring concerns that the nation’s central bank isn’t tightening policy fast enough to quell inflation.

title: Japan Banks With Shipping Finance Expertise Are Latest to Merge
summary: A proposed merger between Iyogin Holdings Inc. and Ehime Bank Ltd. adds to a series of recent M&A activity between Japanese regional lenders as an aging and shrinking population weighs on their business.
